 {"id":"bd-7h7","title":"bd init should stop running daemon to avoid stale cache","description":"When running bd init, any running daemon continues with stale cached data, causing bd stats and other commands to show old counts.

Repro:
1. Have daemon running with 788 issues cached
2. Clean JSONL to 128 issues, delete db, run bd init
3. bd stats still shows 788 (daemon cache)
4. Must manually run bd daemon --stop

Fix: bd init should automatically stop any running daemon before reinitializing.","status":"tombstone","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-16T13:26:47.117226-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T16:11:17.070763-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-17T16:11:17.070763-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"bug"}

 {"id":"bd-7pwh","title":"HOP-compatible schema additions","description":"Add optional fields to Beads schema to enable future HOP integration.
All fields are backwards-compatible (optional, omitted if empty).

## Reference
See ~/gt/docs/hop/BEADS-SCHEMA-CHANGES.md for full specification.

## P1 Changes (Must Have Before Launch)

### 1. EntityRef type
Structured entity reference that can become HOP URI:
```go
type EntityRef struct {
    Name     string // "polecat/Nux"
    Platform string // "gastown"
    Org      string // "steveyegge"  
    ID       string // "polecat-nux"
}
```

### 2. creator field
Every issue tracks who created it (EntityRef).

### 3. assignee_ref field
Structured form alongside existing string assignee.

### 4. validations array
Track who validated work completion:
```go
type Validation struct {
    Validator *EntityRef
    Outcome   string    // accepted, rejected, revision_requested
    Timestamp time.Time
    Score     *float32  // Future
}
```

## P2 Changes (Should Have)

### 5. work_type field
"mutex" (default) or "open_competition"

### 6. crystallizes field
Boolean - does this work compound (true) or evaporate (false)?

### 7. cross_refs field
Array of URIs to beads in other repos:
- "beads://github/anthropics/claude-code/bd-xyz"

## P3 Changes (Nice to Have)

### 8. skill_vector placeholder
Reserved for future embeddings: []float32

## Implementation Notes
- All fields optional in JSONL serialization
- Empty/null fields omit from output
- No migration needed for existing data
- CLI additions: --creator, --validated-by filters","notes":"Scope reduced after review. P1 only: EntityRef type, creator field, validations array. Deferred: assignee_ref, work_type, crystallizes, cross_refs, skill_vector (YAGNI - semantics unclear, can add later when needed).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-22T02:42:39.267984-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T20:09:09.211821-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T20:09:09.211821-08:00","close_reason":"HOP P1 schema additions complete: EntityRef type, Creator field, Validations array. P2/P3 items deferred to bd-zt59."}

 {"id":"bd-lxzx","title":"Add close_reason to JSONL export format documentation","description":"PR #551 now persists close_reason to the database, but there's a question about whether this field should be exported to JSONL format.

## Current State
- close_reason is stored in issues.close_reason column
- close_reason is also stored in events table (audit trail)
- The JSONL export format may or may not include close_reason

## Questions
1. Should close_reason be exported to JSONL format?
2. If yes, where should it go (root level or nested in events)?
3. Should there be any special handling to avoid duplication?
4. How should close_reason be handled during JSONL import?

## Why This Matters
- JSONL is the git-friendly sync format
- Other beads instances import from JSONL
- close_reason is meaningful data that should be preserved across clones

## Suggested Action
- Check if close_reason is currently exported in JSONL
- If not, add it to the export schema
- Document the field in JSONL format spec
- Add tests for round-trip (export -> import -> verify close_reason)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-14T14:25:17.414916-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-14T14:25:17.414916-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lxzx","depends_on_id":"bd-z86n","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-14T14:25:17.416131-08:00","created_by":"stevey","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"bd-lz49","title":"Add gate fields: await_type, await_id, timeout, waiters","description":"Add gate-specific fields to the Issue type.

## New Fields
- await_type: string - Type of condition (gh:run, gh:pr, timer, human, mail)
- await_id: string - Identifier for the condition
- timeout: duration - Max time to wait before escalation
- waiters: []string - Mail addresses to notify when gate clears

## Implementation
- Add fields to Issue struct in internal/types/types.go
- Update SQLite schema for new columns
- Add JSONL serialization/deserialization
- Update import/export logic","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T11:44:32.720196-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T12:00:03.837691-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T12:00:03.837691-08:00","close_reason":"Gate fields added to Issue struct, ComputeContentHash, SQLite migration created, all storage queries updated","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lz49","depends_on_id":"bd-udsi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T11:44:52.738823-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-lz49","depends_on_id":"bd-2v0f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-23T11:44:56.269351-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-m0tl","title":"bd create -f crashes with nil pointer dereference","description":"GitHub issue #674. The markdown import feature crashes at markdown.go:338 because global variables (store, ctx, actor) aren't initialized when createIssuesFromMarkdown is called. The function uses globals set by cobra command framework but is being called before they're ready. Need to either initialize globals at start of function or pass them as parameters.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:35:14.813012-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T15:41:14.600953-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T15:41:14.600953-08:00","close_reason":"Fixed: added nil check for store in createIssuesFromMarkdown"}
+{"id":"bd-m6r3","title":"Refactor: Extract shared getEpicChildren helper for swarm commands","description":"## Context
`getSwarmStatus` and `analyzeEpicForSwarm` in cmd/bd/swarm.go have nearly identical code for:
- Getting epic dependents via GetDependents
- Filtering to parent-child relationships
- Building childIDSet map
- Building dependency maps

## Implementation
1. Extract `getEpicChildren(ctx, store, epicID)` that returns:
   - `[]*types.Issue` - child issues
   - `map[string]bool` - child ID set
   - `map[string][]string` - dependency map (within children)
   - `error`

2. Refactor both `analyzeEpicForSwarm` and `getSwarmStatus` to use it

3. Consider defining a named interface for the store methods:
   ```go
   type SwarmStore interface {
       GetIssue(context.Context, string) (*types.Issue, error)
       GetDependents(context.Context, string) ([]*types.Issue, error)
       GetDependencyRecords(context.Context, string) ([]*types.Dependency, error)
   }
   ```

## Files
- cmd/bd/swarm.go (lines 150-248, 616-682)

## Acceptance
- No duplicated code for fetching epic children
- Both commands produce identical results as before","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-28T21:30:55.55377-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/emma","updated_at":"2025-12-28T21:30:55.55377-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-m964","title":"Consider FTS5 for text search at scale","description":"SearchIssues uses LIKE patterns for text search which can't use indexes.

**Current query (queries.go:1475-1477):**
```sql
(title LIKE ? OR description LIKE ? OR id LIKE ?)
```

**Problem:** Full table scan on every text search. At 100K+ issues, this becomes slow.

**SQLite FTS5 solution:**
```sql
CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE issues_fts USING fts5(
  id, title, description, design, notes,
  content='issues',
  content_rowid='rowid'
);

-- Triggers to keep FTS in sync
CREATE TRIGGER issues_ai AFTER INSERT ON issues BEGIN
  INSERT INTO issues_fts(rowid, id, title, description, design, notes)
  VALUES (new.rowid, new.id, new.title, new.description, new.design, new.notes);
END;
-- (similar for UPDATE, DELETE)
```

**Trade-offs:**
- Database size increase (~30-50% for text content)
- Additional write overhead (trigger execution)
- Better search capabilities (ranking, phrase search)

**Decision needed:** Is full-text search a priority feature? Current LIKE search may be acceptable for most use cases.

**Benchmark first:** Measure SearchIssues at 100K scale before implementing.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-22T22:58:56.466121-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T22:58:56.466121-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m964","depends_on_id":"bd-h0we","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-22T22:58:56.466764-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
